response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a capable full-stack and web engineer with genuine GIS/geospatial exposure through the GISCollective platform and Green Map work, and has dealt with high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic. However, the role fundamentally requires production C++ or Rust systems programming, and the candidate's C++ experience is a brief six-month stint over ten years ago (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015) with no indication of continued use. Their entire career trajectory — and stated skillset — runs through JavaScript, TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no routing, navigation tiling, or data pipeline work at the required depth, and no Rust experience at all.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform development via GISCollective and Green Map GIS application, including a FOSS4G conference talk
Demonstrated experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Polyglot background with exposure to low-level and systems-adjacent languages (DLang, historical C/C++, embedded work at Altom)
concerns: C++ experience is limited to a single 6-month role over 10 years ago; no Rust experience mentioned anywhere in the resume or skills list
No routing, navigation guidance, or geospatial tiling pipeline experience — GIS work is data collection/visualization, not routing or tile generation
Entire recent career (2018–present) is TypeScript/Node.js/web stack; primary skillset is fundamentally misaligned with a systems-level C++ pipeline role
